steps.title: Make the two-row table
steps.content: Use the starting value and rate directly. At birth, $x=0$ and the whale is $160$ cm long. One month later, the length is $160+4.8=164.8$ cm. Use those two points: $(0,160)$ and $(1,164.8)$.

steps.title: Write the linear model
steps.content: The starting value is the $y$-intercept, so $b=160$. The whale grows $4.8$ cm per month, so the slope is $m=4.8$. The model is $$y=4.8x+160.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The slope must be $4.8$ centimeters per month, and the starting value must be $160$ centimeters. Choice D is the only option with both: $y=4.8x+160$. Choice D is correct.
